steps.title: Interpret g(-9) = 0 to constrain the form of f(x)
steps.content:
Since $g(-9) = \frac{f(-9)}{-9+3} = \frac{f(-9)}{-6} = 0$, the numerator must be zero (denominator is nonzero). Thus $f(-9) = 0$.

For linear $f(x) = mx + b$: $$-9m + b = 0$$ $$b = 9m$$

steps.title: Use g(-27) = 3 to find the slope m
Calculate the y-intercept
steps.content: From $b = 9m = 9(4) = 36$, we have $f(x) = 4x + 36$, so $f(0) = 36$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
